yeah i have an agent running dlmm for me. but i still LP manually every day.

because it's a life.

solana-sol, 9 hours, $76, classic bid-ask. nothing fancy. set the range, let fees accumulate, wait for the exit pump, take profit and move on.

that's really the whole strat. a lot of people overthink it looking for the perfect entry, the perfect range, the perfect timing. 

but classic bid-ask on the right token is just patient capital. your range sits there collecting fees from every bin movement and when the pump comes you ride it out clean.

the agent handles the screening and the overnight positions. i handle the plays i actually want to be in myself. both things can exist at the same time.

manual LPing keeps me sharp. i'm still reading charts, still checking volume, still making decisions on token selection with my own eyes. the agent learns from top LPers but i learn from actually being in positions. that feedback loop matters.

$76 for 9 hours of just letting a position breathe. this is why i don't stop doing this manually even when the agent is running.

6 Years of Solana: The Key Milestones 

> Mar 2018: @solana raises its first seed round (~$2M) 

> Jul 2019: Solana raises $20M in a Series A led by Multicoin Capital

> Mar 2020: Solana Mainnet Beta launches

> Apr 2020: SOL begins trading on crypto exchanges

> Jun 2020: Solana surpasses 1B total transactions

> Jun 2021: Solana raises $314M in a private token sale led by Andreessen Horowitz and Polychain

> Aug–Sep 2021: Solana in the top blockchains by market cap

> Nov 2021: SOL reaches an all-time high near $260.

> Jan 2022: Solana surpasses 50B total transactions

> Nov 2022: Google Cloud announces it is running a Solana validator

> Nov 2022: The FTX collapse causes SOL to drop ~50%

> Apr 2023: @solanamobile launches the Saga crypto smartphone

> Aug 2023: Solana Pay integrates with Shopify for USDC payments

> Mar 2024: Solana becomes the #1 blockchain by daily active addresses

> Mar 2025: Solana surpasses 400B transactions

> Aug 2025: Solana Mobile Seeker begins global shipping

> Oct 2025: Bitwise launches the first U.S. Solana ETF

> Jan 2026: The SKR token launches

> Mar 2026: Solana celebrates 6 years since the genesis block.

i've been running overnight classic bid-ask consistently this month and the results have been clean. let me break down exactly how the strategy works from token selection all the way to exit.

the whole strategy lives or dies on token selection. this isn't a setup you can run on any random token and expect to wake up green.

token criteria:
- mcap 500k minimum
- token age at least 2 days old
- at least one credible KOL holding or has been seen in the token
- bubble maps looks clean, no suspicious clustering or wallet concentration

why 2 days minimum? tokens that are less than 48 hours old are still in price discovery. overnight on a day-1 token is gambling, not strategy. you want a token that has already shown it can survive a dip and still have buyers.

entry is after the token hits ath and dumps around -30%. that's the zone.

not at ath. not during the pump. after. when most people are panic selling or already moved on to the next ticker, that's when you open your position.

the setup is classic bid-ask, default range around -50% from entry price, single side sol only.

why not use wide range from ath? capital efficiency. a wide range from ath means most of your bins are sitting unused if price doesn't recover all the way back. 

default range from the -30% dip point means your capital is concentrated where the actual price action is happening. more fees.

exit is either on the pump back up or when profit hits 5%+, whichever comes first. if you wake up and you're already at green, close it.

the honest part: this strategy is entirely about which token you pick. the mechanics are simple, 

the range is standard, the entry zone is clear. but if you pick a token that just bleeds overnight with no recovery, no setup saves you.

get the token right and you'll almost always wake up on profit.

@MeteoraAG @met_lparmy

I’m still a big $JUP holder and staker, and I’m at a loss

Even though it cost me some money, this experience taught me a rule: sell 99% of airdrops. I’ve been following that rule for over a year, and it has saved me far more than I’ve lost

Even when a company is winning, it doesn’t guarantee that holding or staking is worth it - most governance token systems simply don’t work or make no sense

woke up to $107 on rosie-sol. 6 hours overnight, classic bid-ask, clean exit. this is what happens when you enter at the right price.

i entered around -35% from ath. not at the top, not chasing the pump. by the time i opened the position the token had already taken most of its pain and had room to breathe.

that's the whole point of overnight positions. you're not trying to catch the move, you're set your range in an area where the price has already corrected and letting fees do the work while you sleep.

but here's the honest part about overnight. it's not just about the strategy, it's about when you wake up.

if you wake up and the position is red, it sets the tone for your entire day. you start second-guessing, you close early, you miss the recovery. 

that's why i never enter overnight or multidays at ath. entering after a meaningful dip means even if price moves against me overnight, i have a buffer. 

since i didn't like wide positions because of capital efficiency so i always wait for ze dip.

i've been more comfortable running multi-hours and multi-days strats this month than i've been in a long time.

$100 in a single night while sleeping. not a bad way to start the morning.
